pattern: Union-Find groups vertices into components in near-linear time, and a component is complete exactly when its edge count matches the maximum possible for its size.
hints: Union every edge's two endpoints with a disjoint-set (union-find) structure so each vertex ends up tagged with its component's root.
While you union, or in a second pass, tally two numbers per root: how many vertices belong to that component and how many edges landed inside it.
A component with v vertices is complete exactly when its edge count equals v*(v-1)/2 -- the count of all possible pairs -- so compare that formula against your tally for every root.

edge_inputs.description: single vertex, no edges -- trivially complete
fully connected component using every possible edge
one complete pair plus several isolated vertices
a complete triangle plus one isolated vertex

stress.description: random graphs with a random subset of all possible edges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50))
    all_pairs = [(a, b) for a in range(n) for b in range(a + 1, n)]
    rng.shuffle(all_pairs)
    k = rng.randint(0, len(all_pairs))
    edges = [[a, b] for a, b in all_pairs[:k]]
    return [n, edges]
